heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks on Kangaroo Island
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Kangaroo Island? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks on Kangaroo Island so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
KI is the wildlife trip: sea lions at Seal Bay, kangaroos through the campground at dusk and beaches with nobody on them. The ferry booking is the make-or-break detail, so start there.
local_knowledge: Book the SeaLink ferry from Cape Jervis before anything else; vehicle spots in school holidays sell out well ahead, and the crossing to Penneshaw takes 45 minutes. Distances on the island surprise people, Kingscote to Flinders Chase is a solid 90 minutes, so plan one end at a time. Seal Bay's guided beach walk among wild sea lions is the single best family experience on the island. Discovery Parks near Flinders Chase puts the Remarkable Rocks and Admirals Arch on your doorstep, while the Kingscote park is the town-and-beach base with Brownlow Beach across the road. Emu Bay's drive-on beach is the swimming pick, and Little Sahara's sandboarding is the sleeper hit for kids.

if_we_were_booking.label: Wildlife and the wild west end
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Discovery Parks Kangaroo Island for cabins near Flinders Chase, kangaroos through camp at dusk and short runs to Remarkable Rocks and Admirals Arch.

if_we_were_booking.label: Town and beach base
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose KI Caravan Park at Kingscote with Brownlow Beach across the road, shops nearby and wildlife spotting from the park itself.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Board sandboards for Little Sahara and swim at Emu Bay, the locals' beach. And book Seal Bay's guided tour rather than just the boardwalk, walking the beach among wild sea lions is the memory the kids keep.

faq.question: Is Kangaroo Island good for kids?
faq.answer: Excellent for wildlife-mad kids. Wild sea lions at Seal Bay, kangaroos and echidnas around the parks, sandboarding at Little Sahara and calm swimming at Emu Bay.

faq.question: How do you get to Kangaroo Island with a car or van?
faq.answer: The SeaLink ferry from Cape Jervis to Penneshaw, about 45 minutes. Book vehicle spots well ahead for school holidays, they genuinely sell out.

faq.question: Can you take a caravan on the ferry?
faq.answer: Yes, priced by length. Many families leave big vans on the mainland and take the car plus a cabin booking instead, it's often cheaper than shipping the van across.

faq.question: What is the one thing families shouldn't miss?
faq.answer: Seal Bay's guided tour. You walk the beach among a wild sea lion colony with a guide. The boardwalk alone is good, the beach walk is unforgettable.

faq.question: How big is Kangaroo Island really?
faq.answer: Bigger than people expect, 155 kilometres end to end. Kingscote to Flinders Chase is about 90 minutes, so split your stay or accept some driving.

faq.question: Where is the best swimming for kids?
faq.answer: Emu Bay, calm, shallow and you can drive onto the beach. Stokes Bay's hidden beach through the rock tunnel is the adventure option.

faq.question: How many nights do you need?
faq.answer: Three nights minimum, five is comfortable. Day trips from the mainland spend most of the day on the ferry and miss the dusk wildlife, which is the best part.

faq.question: What wildlife will kids actually see?
faq.answer: Kangaroos and wallabies at dusk around the parks, sea lions at Seal Bay, koalas in the trees around Flinders Chase and, with luck, echidnas crossing the road.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: The island is quiet in rain. Seal Bay tours still run, Clifford's Honey Farm and the Emu Ridge eucalyptus distillery are undercover, and cabin board games fill the gaps.

faq.question: Is Kangaroo Island expensive?
faq.answer: The ferry adds real cost for vehicles, and groceries run higher than the mainland. Fuel up and stock up in Kingscote, and budget the ferry as part of the trip cost.
